content: Comparative evaluations of curiosity-driven reinforcement learning methods (ICM vs RND vs hand-designed curious agents) reveal that performance on hard exploration tasks is tightly correlated with how well each method implicitly compresses and retrieves novel unexpected experiences into the agent’s episodic memory, an understudied axis of comparison missing from existing 2024-2025 SOTA active learning agent benchmarks. This leads to the hypothesis that standard intrinsic reward metrics for curiosity can be replaced with a memory-aware intrinsic reward that scores states based on how much they reshape the agent’s existing episodic memory topology, outperforming both ICM and RND on sparse-reward long-horizon tasks.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
